framework: Schelling focal point detection + physical observables
design: 两层观测: 物理层(状态)+ 焦点层(转折). History in geopulse.db

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo CONFIRMED one-off, US struck Iran's largest bridge Apr 2 (8 killed 95 wounded)
history.note: Day 44. ★★★★★ BRIDGE STRIKE + SUSTAINED MULTI-FRONT: (1) US/Israel STRUCK Iran's largest bridge near Karaj Apr 2 — 8 killed, 95 wounded, Trump: 'more to follow' (Guardian/BBC Apr 2); (2) CENTCOM Apr 2 video: strikes on drones/tanks/missile launchers, 12,300+ sites struck week 5 (AJE/CENTCOM); (3) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — mass salvo era over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah = ONE-OFF coordinated show-of-force; (4) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM toward Beersheba (Wikipedia Apr 2); (5) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (6) CENTCOM: 155 Iranian vessels damaged/destroyed; (7) US KIA: 13 (Pentagon/NYT) to 15 (Wikipedia) + 520+ wounded (Intercept: Pentagon COVERING UP figures); (8) Iran: HRANA 3,486+ killed; (9) WTI SPIKED 11% to USD111.54, Brent USD109.03 on Trump Apr 2 'extremely hard' speech; (10) Trump vows 'extremely hard 2-3 weeks' WHILE claiming 'nearing completion'. PATTERN: Infrastructure targeting ESCALATING (bridge = civilian infrastructure). 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign week 5 FAILING to eliminate Iran strike capability (Hegseth concession). Source: Guardian/BBC/AJE/CENTCOM/CNBC/NYT/Intercept/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo confirmed one-off, US struck largest bridge Karaj Apr 2 (8 killed 95 wounded), CENTCOM Wk5 12,300 sites
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ WEEK 5 SUSTAINED MULTI-FRONT + INFRASTRUCTURE ESCALATION: (1) CENTCOM Apr 2: 12,300 sites struck in 5 weeks, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates/AJE); (2) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — mass salvo era CONFIRMED over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ heavy missiles+drones WITH Houthis+Hezbollah = ONE-OFF coordinated show-of-force (Moneycontrol/TheHindu); (3) US struck Iran LARGEST BRIDGE near Karaj Apr 2 — 8 killed, 95 wounded, Trump: 'more to follow' (Guardian/BBC Apr 2) = CIVILIAN INFRASTRUCTURE targeting while claiming wind-down; (4) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM toward Beersheba Apr 2, vowed to continue (Wikipedia); (5) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (6) Brent SURGED past USD109 Apr 3 after Trump speech (BBC); (7) Trump Apr 2 vows 'extremely hard 2-3 weeks' WHILE claiming near-victory = MAXIMUM PARADOX; (8) Hegseth STILL concedes Iran retains strike ability despite monthlong bombing (NYT); (9) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded, Pentagon COVERING UP (Intercept); (10) Iran: HRANA 3,486+ killed. ★★★ CRITICAL: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign week 5 FAILING to eliminate capability. Apr 6 energy deadline 3 DAYS. Source: BBC/Guardian/VoiceOfEmirates/AJE/NYT/CNBC/Intercept/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline) BUT IRGC claims 2nd F-35 shot down over central Tehran Apr 3 2:50AM; multi-front fire exchange SUSTAINED; explosions Tehran+Isfahan
history.note: Day 35/Day 45. ★★★★★ IRGC 2ND F-35 CLAIM + MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED: (1) IRGC claims SHOT DOWN 2nd US F-35 over central Tehran at 2:50AM Apr 3 — 'advanced modern aerospace defense system' (24NewsHD/News18/HindustanTimes); (2) Wikipedia: Raad AD system or adapted air-to-air missile (Chinese analysts via SCMP); (3) Pentagon has NOT confirmed — first F-35 claim also disputed; (4) US intel warns Iran STILL has 'significant missile-launching capability' and can 'wreak absolute havoc' (Moneycontrol Apr 3); (5) Iran+allies TRADED FIRE with Israel/US Apr 3 — Washington-linked assets across West Asia targeted incl energy+civilian infrastructure (TheHindu/Outlook Apr 3); (6) Attacks increasingly focusing on economic/industrial sites (Outlook); (7) US/Israel struck century-old medical research centre Tehran + steel plants + bridge (Moneycontrol Apr 3); (8) Explosions in Tehran + Isfahan Friday (HindustanTimes); (9) Iran fired missiles at Israel + Gulf nations (HindustanTimes); (10) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' BUT Hegseth concedes Iran retains strike ability; (11) Houthis SUSTAINED — independent BM launches; (12) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT. ★★★ CRITICAL: IRGC F-35 claim (if verified) = Iran AD NOT eliminated after 5 weeks bombing. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts confirmed active. Multi-front fire exchange ESCALATING. Source: 24NewsHD/News18/HindustanTimes/Moneycontrol/TheHindu/Outlook/Wikipedia/SCMP

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), IRGC claims 2nd F-35 shot down Apr 3, 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained, CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ IRGC F-35 CLAIM + IRAN-OMAN PERMANENT PROTOCOL: (1) IRGC claims 2nd F-35 shot down over central Tehran Apr 3 2:50AM — Raad AD system or adapted air-to-air missile (SCMP Chinese analysts); Pentagon NOT confirmed; (2) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates Apr 2); (3) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah CONFIRMED one-off; (4) US intel: Iran STILL has 'significant missile-launching capability', can 'wreak absolute havoc' (Moneycontrol Apr 3); (5) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM launches at Israel, threatening Bab el-Mandeb closure if escalation continues (TWZ/Wikipedia Apr 2-3); (6) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT front; (7) US struck Karaj bridge Apr 2 (8 killed, 95 wounded) + medical center + steel plants = CIVILIAN infrastructure targeting (Guardian/BBC/Moneycontrol); (8) Iran+allies TRADED FIRE with Israel/US Apr 3 — energy+civilian infrastructure targeted (TheHindu/Outlook); (9) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded (Intercept: Pentagon COVERING UP); (10) Iran: HRANA 7,007+ deaths documented. ★★★ CRITICAL: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign Wk5 FAILING to eliminate Iran strike capability (Hegseth concession). IRGC F-35 claim = Iran AD NOT destroyed. Source: 24NewsHD/News18/SCMP/VoiceOfEmirates/Moneycontrol/TWZ/Wikipedia/Guardian/BBC/Intercept/TheHindu/Outlook

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ US F-15E CONFIRMED shot down over Iran Apr 3 — first US aircraft loss, S&R underway, pilot fate unknown; IRGC claimed F-35 but wreckage shows F-15E tail sections (TWZ); multi-front fire exchange SUSTAINED
history.note: Day 36/Day 46. ★★★★★ US F-15E CONFIRMED SHOT DOWN + MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED: (1) ★★★ BREAKING: US F-15E fighter jet CONFIRMED shot down over Iran Apr 3 — NYT/Reuters/CBS/Guardian all confirm; US S&R mission underway, pilot fate unknown; IRGC claimed F-35 but analysts ID wreckage as F-15E tail sections (TWZ/TurkiyeToday); (2) AP: Iran state TV affiliate says pilot ejected over southwestern Iran, locals converging on crash site, IRGC calls on public not to mistreat pilot; (3) Tasnim (IRGC-linked): US rescue attempts have FAILED; (4) CBS: low-altitude US aircraft seen over hostile territory = rescue ops; (5) NYT: US death toll 13 combat + 2 non-combat = 15 KIA + hundreds wounded; (6) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates); (7) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day; Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah = one-off; (8) Houthis SUSTAINED — independent BM launches at Israel; (9) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (10) Iran+allies TRADED FIRE across Mideast Apr 3, targets incl energy+civilian infrastructure (AP/TheMessenger). ★★★ CRITICAL: F-15E shootdown = Iran AD NOT eliminated after 5+ weeks bombing. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts confirmed active. First confirmed US aircraft loss = massive political shock. Source: NYT/Reuters/CBS/Guardian/TWZ/AP/TurkiyeToday/VoiceOfEmirates/Tasnim

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ F-15E CONFIRMED shot down Apr 3 = first US aircraft loss; one pilot rescued, one missing; CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites; Apr 2 IRGC intercepted 26 drones+9 BM (UAE) + 13 drones+2 CM (Kuwait) + more (Bahrain); 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained
history.note: Day 46. ★★★★★ F-15E LOSS + MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED: (1) ★★★ BREAKING: US F-15E shot down over Iran Apr 3 — NYT confirms one pilot rescued, other missing; IRGC claimed F-35 but analysts ID F-15E (TWZ); (2) ISW Apr 2: Emirati MoD intercepted 26 drones+9 BM; Kuwait 13 drones+2 CM; Bahrain intercepted drones+BM — coordinated IRGC multi-front salvo vs Gulf bases Apr 2; (3) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' BUT F-15E loss contradicts; (4) IRGC baseline 1-2/day RESIDUAL but coordinated salvos PERSIST; (5) Houthis SUSTAINED — independent BM launches at Israel; (6) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (7) US KIA: 13-15 confirmed + hundreds wounded (NYT/Wikipedia/Intercept); (8) Iran: HRANA 7,007+ deaths documented; (9) Trump: '2-3 more extremely hard weeks'; (10) Iran AD NOT eliminated — F-15E shootdown proves capability retained. PATTERN: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual+coordinated salvos, Houthis sustained, Hezbollah 5K+). F-15E loss = biggest US military shock since war start. Source: NYT/Reuters/CBS/Guardian/ISW/TWZ/Wikipedia/Intercept

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ TWO US AIRCRAFT LOST Apr 3: F-15E shot down over western Iran (494th FS) + A-10 Warthog shot down near Hormuz (Persian Gulf); 2 CSAR helicopters also hit; F-15E pilot rescued, WSO MISSING — IRGC/Army AD claims; Israel SUSPENDED airstrikes for rescue ops; Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal via third country; CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites struck but Iran AD proven NOT eliminated
history.note: Day 47. ★★★★★ DOUBLE AIRCRAFT LOSS = PARADIGM SHIFT: (1) F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) shot down over western Iran — pilot rescued, WSO missing, IRGC/Army AD claims, ejection seat found near crash (Wikipedia/TWZ/WaPo/NYT/CBS/NBC); (2) A-10 Warthog ALSO shot down near Hormuz/Persian Gulf same timeframe — pilot rescued (NYT/WaPo/Forbes/Guardian); (3) TWO CSAR helicopters HIT during rescue ops, crews injured but returned to base (WaPo); (4) Israel SUSPENDED airstrikes in areas 'relevant' to rescue (CBS/CNBCTV18); (5) Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal via third country (Reuters/Fars/France24); (6) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' BUT double shootdown CONTRADICTS; (7) UNSC Hormuz vote FURTHER DELAYED to next week (Reuters); China opposes force authorization; (8) ★★★ FIRST WESTERN HORMUZ TRANSITS: French CMA CGM Kribi + Japanese tanker + Omani vessels crossed Hormuz Apr 3 — first Western European transit since war (Bloomberg/Reuters/AJE/Guardian/Euronews); (9) 3 Omani tankers used NEW Oman coast route BYPASSING Iranian corridor (Bloomberg); (10) Good Friday — US stock markets CLOSED. Source: NYT/WaPo/Reuters/CBS/NBC/Forbes/TWZ/Bloomberg/AJE/Guardian/Euronews/France24

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), Iran drafting PERMANENT Hormuz protocol with Oman, Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED, 5 friendly nations + Malaysia + Philippines expanding, NO US escort
history.note: Day 44. ★★★★★ IRAN PERMANENT SOVEREIGNTY BID: (1) ★★★ NYT Apr 2: Iran Deputy FM Gharibabadi says Iran drafting PROTOCOL with Oman to 'oversee transit through Hormuz' EVEN IN PEACETIME — most significant sovereignty escalation yet; (2) NYT: Malaysia + Philippines announced Iran promised to allow their vessels = friendly-nation list EXPANDING beyond original 5 (CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (3) CNBC Apr 2: Iran INSTITUTIONALIZED Larak Island checkpoint — IRGC vets each ship (IMO/cargo/crew), issues corridor code + 20-mile channel under IRGC escort; (4) Bloomberg: yuan + crypto tolls — Tehran tollbooth formalized; (5) Iran parliament CODIFIED Hormuz tolls legislation; (6) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts of commercial shipping' — CONFIRMED; (7) Tankers STILL fleeing Dubai post-Al-Salmi VLCC attack; (8) ~3,000 vessels waiting; (9) USNI/Lloyd's: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 same period 2025 = 94.6% decline UNCHANGED. ★★★ CRITICAL: Iran moving from WARTIME blockade to PERMANENT peacetime oversight regime. Two-tier Hormuz HARDENING: friendly+expanding nations transit with tolls+IRGC escort, Western/hostile BLOCKED. Still 95% below pre-crisis. Source: NYT/CNBC/Bloomberg/USNI/AA/Guardian

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day, Polymarket 98.3% for 0-10 ships Apr 3), Iran EXPANDING friendly-nation list (PH Apr 2), Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED, NO US escort
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ IRAN EXPANDING TOLL REGIME + TRANSIT STILL NEAR-ZERO: (1) HinduBusinessLine Apr 2: transit COLLAPSED from 150/day to 4-5/day — CONFIRMED; (2) Polymarket Apr 3: 98.3% probability of 0-10 ships today; (3) Iran EXPANDING friendly-nation list: Philippines Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK + Malaysia + Thailand) — widening two-tier system (Wikipedia/Philstar); (4) Most crossings follow Iran-approved routes (Philstar Apr 2); (5) Iran FORMALIZED Larak Island checkpoint — IRGC vets each ship (IMO/cargo/crew), USD2M toll, yuan+crypto (CNBC/HouseOfSaud); (6) Iran parliament CODIFIED Hormuz tolls legislation; (7) Iran-Oman PERMANENT Hormuz oversight protocol being drafted (NYT Apr 2); (8) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — CONFIRMED 5 weeks in; (9) Tankers still fleeing Dubai post Al-Salmi VLCC attack; (10) 2,190 vessels with transponders in Gulf west of Hormuz (Bloomberg/Philstar) — massive backlog. PATTERN: Two-tier Hormuz HARDENING into PERMANENT regime. Friendly-nation list expanding but Western shipping BLOCKED. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis 120 transits/day. Source: HinduBusinessLine/Polymarket/Philstar/Wikipedia/CNBC/USNI/Bloomberg/NYT

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), Bloomberg: 3 Omani tankers enter via NEW Oman coast route BYPASSING Iranian corridor, Iran warns vs UN Hormuz force vote
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ OMAN BYPASS ROUTE EMERGES + UN HORMUZ VOTE: (1) ★★★ Bloomberg Apr 2: 3 Omani-owned tankers entered Hormuz via NEW ROUTE hugging Oman coastline — NOT using Iranian corridor/Larak checkpoint = FIRST crack in Iran's toll monopoly; (2) Iran warns against 'provocative action' ahead of UN vote on Hormuz force (TheHindu Apr 3); (3) Polymarket still 98%+ for 0-10 ships/day; (4) HinduBusinessLine: transit collapsed from 150/day to 4-5/day — confirmed; (5) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets each ship, USD2M toll, yuan+crypto; (6) Friendly-nation list EXPANDING: Philippines added Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK/MY/TH); (7) Iran-Oman PERMANENT Hormuz oversight protocol in drafting (NYT Apr 2); (8) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks unfulfilled; (9) Russian crude Urals surged to USD123.45 as Gulf flows curtailed (GulfNews Apr 3); (10) ~2,190 vessels with transponders waiting west of Hormuz (Bloomberg). ★★★ TWO DEVELOPMENTS: (A) Oman coastal bypass = potential alternative to Iranian corridor; (B) UN Hormuz force vote approaching = multilateral pressure. But still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: Bloomberg/TheHindu/HinduBusinessLine/Polymarket/USNI/GulfNews/NYT

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), Iran-Oman drafting PERMANENT Hormuz 'monitoring' protocol, Windward Apr 1: ~11 ships (1T+4C in / 1T+5C out), NO US escort Wk5+
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ IRAN PERMANENT SOVEREIGNTY ESCALATION: (1) ★★★ CNBC/IRNA Apr 3: Iran+Oman drafting protocol to 'monitor transit' through Hormuz — Deputy FM Gharibabadi: tanker traffic 'should be supervised and coordinated' with Iran+Oman = PERMANENT peacetime control bid; (2) MS.NOW: Iran moves to take 'permanent control' of Hormuz; (3) Windward Apr 1 daily: 1 tanker + 4 cargo inbound, 1 tanker + 5 cargo outbound = ~11 ships/day through Hormuz (vs 150/day pre-crisis); (4) UANI Apr 1: OFAC-sanctioned tankers transiting with AIS off; (5) Friendly-nation list EXPANDING: PH/MY/TH added (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (6) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets+escorts each ship, USD2M toll, yuan+crypto; (7) Iran parliament CODIFIED tolls legislation; (8) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks; (9) FPRI Apr 2: Hormuz campaign requires 1-2 warships per escort + mine countermeasures; (10) 31st MEU deployed but NO escorts materialized. ★★★ TWO-TIER HARDENING INTO PERMANENT REGIME: Iran-Oman protocol = attempt to codify wartime control into peacetime international arrangement. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: CNBC/IRNA/MS.NOW/Windward/UANI/USNI/FPRI/Wikipedia

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED to Saturday, Bahrain waters down to 'defensive only', Russia/China/France opposed force, Iran-Oman permanent protocol drafting
history.note: Day 46. ★★★★★ UNSC HORMUZ VOTE DELAYED + DEFENSIVE ONLY: (1) AP: Bahrain WATERS DOWN UNSC Hormuz resolution — from 'all necessary means' to 'all DEFENSIVE means necessary and commensurate' = NO offensive military action authorized (AP Apr 3); (2) DW: UNSC vote DELAYED from Friday to Saturday (originally Apr 4 → Apr 5); (3) Russia/China/France all opposed original force authorization — China: 'unlawful and indiscriminate'; Russia: 'does not solve the puzzle'; France: indicated watered-down version 'might be acceptable'; (4) Final draft: 6-month defensive mandate, countries must notify UNSC in advance; (5) DW: ship transit 'slightly up but still far below pre-war levels'; (6) Polymarket: 98.3% for 0-10 ships Apr 3; (7) Iran-Oman PERMANENT monitoring protocol still in drafting (CNBC/IRNA); (8) Philippines added to friendly-nation list Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK/MY/TH); (9) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll; (10) USNI: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks. ★★★ UNSC vote = first multilateral action attempt but WATERED DOWN. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: AP/DW/CNBC/IRNA/Polymarket/USNI

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED to Sat, Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only', Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz protocol drafting, NO US escort Wk5+
history.note: Day 46. ★★★★★ HORMUZ VOTE DELAYED + IRAN-OMAN PROTOCOL: (1) DW: UNSC vote DELAYED to Saturday — Bahrain resolution WATERED DOWN from 'all necessary means' to 'all defensive means' (AP Apr 3); Russia/China/France opposed force; France may accept defensive version; (2) Iran-Oman drafting PERMANENT 'monitoring' protocol for Hormuz transit = peacetime sovereignty bid (CNBC/IRNA); (3) Gulf Business/Lloyd's: 181 vessels total Mar 1-30 vs ~4,500 same period 2025 = 96% collapse; (4) USNI: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 same period 2025; (5) Friendly-nation list expanding: PH/MY/TH added (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (6) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll; (7) Oman coast bypass route emerged (Bloomberg); (8) Iran-Oman protocol + UNSC vote Sat = competing frameworks; (9) USNI: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks; (10) S&P VOO closed +1.5% on Iran-Oman Hormuz shipping protocol news. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: AP/DW/GulfBusiness/Lloyd's/USNI/CNBC/IRNA/Bloomberg/TipRanks

history.est: ~3-5 mbd (5-8 ships/day INCREASING), ★★★ FIRST WESTERN TRANSITS: French CMA CGM Kribi + Japanese-owned tanker crossed Hormuz Apr 3 = first Western European transit since war; 3 Omani tankers via NEW coast bypass route; friendly-nation list expanding (PH/MY/TH); Iran Larak checkpoint + USD2M toll STILL enforced; UNSC vote delayed to next week
history.note: Day 47. ★★★★★ WESTERN TRANSITS = PARTIAL CRACK IN BLOCKADE: (1) ★★★ Bloomberg/Reuters/AJE/Guardian Apr 3: French CMA CGM Kribi (Maltese-flagged) transited Hormuz EASTBOUND from Dubai — FIRST Western European vessel since war began; (2) Japanese-owned tanker also crossed (Bloomberg); (3) 3 Omani-owned tankers (Dhalkut, Habrut + Sohar LNG) used NEW route hugging Oman coastline BYPASSING Iranian corridor (Bloomberg Apr 2); (4) Macron: only diplomacy not military can open Hormuz — CMA CGM transit = French diplomatic signal; (5) Friendly-nation list EXPANDED: Philippines Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK/MY/TH); (6) Iran Larak checkpoint STILL operating — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll, yuan/crypto; (7) UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED AGAIN to next week (Reuters Apr 3) — China opposes force; Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only'; (8) Iran-Oman permanent monitoring protocol STILL in drafting (CNBC/IRNA); (9) Transit still 95%+ below pre-crisis 150/day BUT trend = incrementally EXPANDING; (10) Ships ~2,190+ waiting west of Hormuz (Bloomberg). Source: Bloomberg/Reuters/AJE/Guardian/Euronews/CNBC/IRNA

q3_spr_release_rate.label: SPR实际释放速率(mbd)
q3_spr_release_rate.delivery_timeline: 120 days per DOE, first barrels end of W3

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE/Wright), D+28, deliveries ACTIVE since Apr 1, NEW 10M Bryan Mound RFP due Apr 6, IEA warns April crunch WORSENING
history.note: Day 44 D+28. ★★★★ SPR ACTIVE BUT OIL SPIKE NEGATES: (1) SPR deliveries UNDERWAY since Apr 1 — Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor receiving (Argus/IndexBox); (2) DOE NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound emergency exchange RFP due Apr 6 (Reuters/DOE); (3) Rate confirmed 1-1.5 mbd (Wright/S&P Global CERAWeek); (4) G7 coordinated 412M bbl release (US 172M = bulk); (5) IEA Birol warns supply crunch WORSENING April (CNBC); (6) ★★★ WTI SPIKED 11% to USD111.54, Brent USD109.03 on Trump speech — ERASES de-escalation pricing (CNBC Apr 2); (7) Gas USD4.06/gal national (AAA/SmartAsset), CA USD5.87+ (Time); (8) Exchange structure: companies repay 20% more barrels Nov 2026-Sep 2028; (9) SPR 'drop in bucket' vs sustained Gulf loss (FinancialContent); (10) OPEC+ production unwind Apr 1. SPR trickle IRRELEVANT — 1.5 mbd vs 15-17 mbd Hormuz disruption = 10% replacement. Oil SPIKING despite SPR deliveries starting. Source: CNBC/Reuters/DOE/Argus/IndexBox/AAA/IEA

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE/Wright), D+29, deliveries ACTIVE since Apr 1, NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound RFP due Apr 6, DOE denies further releases, SPR ~243M bbl projected
history.note: Day 45 D+29. ★★★★ SPR ACTIVE + NEW EXCHANGE + DOE CAPPING: (1) SPR deliveries UNDERWAY since Apr 1 — Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor receiving first barrels (Argus/IndexBox/Reuters); (2) DOE NEW 10M bbl emergency exchange RFP from Bryan Mound Apr 1, proposals due Apr 6 (Reuters/DOE) — exchange not sale, companies repay 20% more barrels Nov 2026-Sep 2028 (EnergiasMedia); (3) Rate confirmed 1-1.5 mbd (Wright/S&P Global); (4) SPR projected to decline from ~415M to ~243M bbl = lowest since early 1980s, ~34% of 714M capacity (Grokipedia); (5) WebProNews: DOE FIRMLY DENIED plans for further releases — near 40-year lows; (6) G7 coordinated 412M bbl release (US 172M = bulk); (7) IEA Birol warns supply crunch WORSENING in April (CNBC); (8) Gas USD4/gal national (USAToday Apr 1), CA well over USD5 (NYT); (9) Brent SURGED past USD109 Apr 3 (BBC), spot cargo hit USD141.36 (CNBC Apr 2) = highest since 2008; (10) WTI ~USD105-110. SPR trickle IRRELEVANT — 1.5 mbd vs 15-17 mbd Hormuz disruption = 10% replacement. Oil SPIKING despite SPR deliveries. Source: Reuters/DOE/EnergiasMedia/Grokipedia/WebProNews/USAToday/BBC/CNBC/IEA

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE/Wright), D+30, deliveries ACTIVE since Apr 1, 10M bbl Bryan Mound RFP due Apr 6, DOE denies further releases, SPR ~243M bbl projected
history.note: Day 45 D+30. ★★★★ SPR ACTIVE BUT IRRELEVANT VS SUPPLY GAP: (1) SPR deliveries UNDERWAY since Apr 1 — Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor receiving (Argus/IndexBox); (2) DOE NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound emergency exchange RFP due Apr 6 (Reuters/DOE) — proposals close same day as energy strike deadline; (3) Rate confirmed 1-1.5 mbd (Wright/S&P Global); (4) SPR projected to decline from ~415M to ~243M bbl = near 40-year low (Grokipedia); (5) DOE FIRMLY DENIED further releases beyond current plan (WebProNews); (6) G7 coordinated 412M bbl release (US 172M = bulk); (7) IEA Birol warns supply crunch WORSENING in April (CNBC); (8) ★★★ Brent spot cargo hit USD141.36 = highest since 2008 (CNBC Apr 2); (9) Gas USD4.06/gal national (SmartAsset/AAA), CA over USD5 (NYT); (10) Exchange structure: companies repay 20% more barrels Nov 2026-Sep 2028; (11) Russian Urals crude surged to USD123.45 as alternative supplier (GulfNews Apr 3). SPR trickle IRRELEVANT — 1.5 mbd vs 15-17 mbd Hormuz disruption = 10% replacement. Spot market pricing EXTREME scarcity. Source: Reuters/DOE/Argus/IndexBox/CNBC/GulfNews/SmartAsset/WebProNews/IEA

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE/Wright), D+30, deliveries ACTIVE since Apr 1, NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound exchange RFP due Apr 6, SPR declining to ~243M bbl (40yr low)

fp1_china_action.label: 中国实质行动(唯一可行聚点注入)
fp1_china_action.status: ACTIVE BLOCKER — China OPPOSES UNSC Hormuz force as 'unlawful'; UNSC vote delayed to NEXT WEEK (Reuters); China-Pakistan 5-pt plan exists but China denies mediator role; France: diplomacy-only stance enabled CMA CGM transit; NO substantive Chinese military/economic ACTION
fp1_china_action.findings: Day 47. ★★★★ CHINA BLOCKING UNSC + FRANCE DIPLOMACY-FIRST: (1) Reuters Apr 3: UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED TO NEXT WEEK — China opposes force authorization as 'unlawful'; (2) Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only' but STILL faces China/Russia opposition; (3) China-Pakistan 5-pt plan exists (CNN Apr 1) but China denies mediator role; (4) France: Macron said ONLY diplomacy can open Hormuz — same day CMA CGM Kribi transited = French diplomatic signal; (5) UNSC roadblock: Russia, China, France all opposed original force language (NYT Apr 2); (6) ★★★ Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal via third country (Reuters/Fars) — mediation NOT working; (7) Iran-Oman bilateral protocol BYPASSES China framework entirely; (8) Zarif formula (nuclear+Hormuz for sanctions) still on table but from ex-official not government; (9) No Chinese naval deployment, no sanctions action, no material Chinese moves; (10) IntelDrop: 'economic considerations preoccupy Beijing'. ASSESSMENT: China = BLOCKER at UNSC + passive mediator. France emerging as independent diplomatic track. Source: Reuters/NYT/CNN/IntelDrop/AJE/Guardian

fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.label: 美方信号包内部矛盾(承诺解锁前导)
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.status: MAXIMUM PARADOX + DOUBLE AIRCRAFT LOSS — ★★★ F-15E + A-10 BOTH shot down Apr 3 = 2 aircraft lost in one day + 2 CSAR helos hit; WSO MISSING (potential POW); Israel SUSPENDED strikes for rescue; Iran REJECTED 48hr ceasefire; Trump 'nearing completion' narrative SHATTERED; NO Hormuz escort Wk5+; Apr 6 energy deadline 2 DAYS; UNSC vote delayed to next week
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.findings: Day 47. ★★★★★ DOUBLE SHOOTDOWN = MAXIMUM CONTRADICTION: (1) ★★★ TWO US COMBAT AIRCRAFT lost Apr 3: F-15E shot down over western Iran (pilot rescued, WSO missing), A-10 Warthog shot down near Hormuz/Persian Gulf (pilot rescued) (NYT/WaPo/CBS/NBC/Forbes); (2) TWO CSAR helicopters ALSO HIT during rescue, crews injured (WaPo); (3) Israel SUSPENDED airstrikes in 'relevant' areas for rescue (CBS/CNBCTV18); (4) Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire via third country (Reuters/Fars/France24) — NO negotiation channel working; (5) Trump 'nearing completion' + 'extremely hard 2-3 weeks' = CONTRADICTION now COMPOUNDED by worst single-day aircraft losses; (6) ZERO US Hormuz escorts 5+ weeks (USNI confirmed); (7) Apr 6 energy strike deadline 2 DAYS + Bryan Mound 10M RFP due same day; (8) UNSC Hormuz vote delayed to NEXT WEEK (Reuters); (9) BUT: French CMA CGM + Japanese tanker transited = PARALLEL de-escalation track via diplomacy; (10) WSO missing = potential POW/hostage crisis → highest political risk yet. Source: NYT/WaPo/CBS/NBC/Forbes/Reuters/USNI/France24

fp3_face_saving_framework.label: 面子工程原材料出现
fp3_face_saving_framework.status: ZARIF FORMULA + 48HR CEASEFIRE REJECTED — Iran REJECTED US 48hr ceasefire via third country (Reuters/Fars); Zarif formula (nuclear+Hormuz for sanctions) still notable but ex-official; Bahrain UNSC watered down + DELAYED to next week; Iran demands: reparations + base withdrawal + non-attack guarantee; Apr 6 energy deadline 2 DAYS; French diplomatic transit = new soft track
fp3_face_saving_framework.findings: Day 47. ★★★★★ CEASEFIRE REJECTION + COMPETING FRAMEWORKS: (1) ★★★ Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal conveyed via third country on Wednesday (Reuters/Fars/France24/Al-Monitor/JPost); (2) Iran demands: war reparations, US base withdrawal from ME, guarantee not to attack again, Lebanon inclusion (DW/Wikipedia); (3) Zarif formula (Foreign Affairs Apr 3): nuclear limits + reopen Hormuz for END ALL sanctions — notable but Zarif = ex-official not current govt; (4) Bahrain UNSC resolution WATERED DOWN to 'defensive only' AND delayed to next week (Reuters); (5) China/Russia oppose even defensive resolution; France may accept; (6) ★★★ FRENCH DIPLOMATIC TRACK: CMA CGM transit SAME DAY Macron said diplomacy-only — de facto bilateral bypass; (7) Japan parallel track via tanker transit; (8) Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz protocol still in drafting; (9) China-Pakistan 5-pt plan = words only; (10) Apr 6 energy strike deadline 2 DAYS = BINARY. ASSESSMENT: ALL formal ceasefire tracks FAILED. But bilateral transit diplomacy (France/Japan/Oman) = soft de-escalation emerging OUTSIDE formal frameworks. Source: Reuters/Fars/France24/DW/Bloomberg/AJE

fp4_cost_threshold.label: 成本阈值的物理逼近
fp4_cost_threshold.status: THRESHOLD BREACHED ALL AXES — ★★★ 2 aircraft lost in ONE DAY (F-15E + A-10) + 2 CSAR helos hit + WSO MISSING = worst single-day US losses; Gas USD4.08 (+USD1.08/month); Brent USD107-112, WTI USD111.29 INVERTED; spot USD141.36 (2008 high); US KIA 15+ hundreds wounded; Good Friday markets closed; UNSC delayed; Apr 6 energy deadline 2 DAYS
fp4_cost_threshold.findings: Day 47. ★★★★★ QUINTUPLE CONVERGENCE: (1) ★★★ 2 US combat aircraft LOST in single day: F-15E over Iran + A-10 near Hormuz + 2 CSAR helos hit = worst single-day US air losses since Vietnam-era; (2) WSO MISSING over Iran = potential POW/hostage crisis (NYT/WaPo/CBS); (3) Gas USD4.08/gal national = highest since Aug 2022, +USD1.08 in one month (AAA), CA well over USD5 (NYT); (4) Brent USD107.57-112.42, WTI USD111.29 INVERTED above Brent = unprecedented accessible-supply repricing; spot cargo USD141.36 = 2008 high (OilPrice/Fortune/CNBC); (5) Gold ~USD4,690+ (JMBullion); (6) S&P 500 last close ~5,670 (Apr 2 — Investopedia: 'snapped 5-week skid'); VIX ~23.87 (Apr 2); (7) Good Friday Apr 3 — US equity markets CLOSED; oil futures active; (8) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded (Wikipedia/Intercept); (9) UNSC vote delayed to next week — no multilateral relief; (10) Apr 6 energy deadline 2 DAYS + Bryan Mound RFP due. ★★★ QUADRUPLE: (A) worst single-day aircraft losses, (B) WSO MIA/potential POW, (C) oil at 2008-level spot, (D) Apr 6 deadline IMMINENT. Source: NYT/WaPo/CBS/Forbes/AAA/OilPrice/Fortune/CNBC/Wikipedia/Intercept

noise_filter.rule: 按行为类型分类,不按人物分类
noise: 巴盖伊等发言人声明(言辞)
媒体'消息人士'报道(未验证)
signal: Trump签署行政令/军事命令(行动)
美军实际调兵/部署变化(行动)
IRGC实际攻击模式变化(行动)
中国实际经济/军事动作(行动)
noise_filter.key_distinction: 同一个人的言辞=噪声,行动=信号。Trump说'just for fun'=噪声。Trump命令打Kharg=信号。

pipeline_daily_checklist: 1. 物理层: 更新Q1(袭击频率) + Q2(通行量) + Q3(SPR释放)
2. 焦点层: 扫描fp1(中国动作) + fp2(美方矛盾) + fp3(退出框架) + fp4(成本阈值)
3. 噪声过滤: 新闻分类为action vs rhetoric
4. 结构性验证: 跑structural_verify检查盲区+停滞+链断裂
5. DAG更新: 只有物理层变化或焦点层信号触发才更新概率
